programmes.show_description: Dockers: A fine ensemble cast, including Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman, star in the detective drama. The team probe the death of a unionist suspected of embezzlement.
The government plans to close down small hospitals in favour of larger establishments. Dr Turner and Shelagh prepare for an inspection that will decide the maternity home's fate.
What Can be Saved: Miep and Jan's worst fear comes true when the Nazis raid The Annex. However, Miep refuses to lose hope and is determined to save the Franks and others at all costs.
Mick and Shazia investigate the discovery of a Viking hoard. Museum curator Rosa Conaghan is suspicious about the circumstances and summons Mick for a second opinion.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Baby Come Back: More jokes about Essex girls in the popular sitcom. When Sharon meets Dave, the telephone engineer and old flame, her thoughts turn to marriage and motherhood.
Up in Smoke: Part 2: The relief have to cover for Jim Carver yet again when he arrives at the station still very drunk.
New. Hidden Demons: Michelle and Nick visit the Campus surgery - and despite finding it tatty and in chaos, decide to make a bid for it. Meanwhile, George comforts a patient.
New. Standing Up: A disabled woman makes a shocking discovery about her carer, while George is less than pleased when the results of her STI test are delayed.
Night Fever: Jess is shocked to find Robert waiting inside her flat for her when she gets home, and the situation turns nasty, while at the hospital Mubbs shows his disgust at Owen.
Into the Fog: As the staff prepare for the cleanliness inspection, Lenny makes several mistakes that jeopardise the ED's chances of success.
Up in Smoke: Part 2: The relief have to cover for Jim Carver yet again when he arrives at the station still very drunk.
New. A stressed Lucas stops Sugar from uncovering his wicked deed, and Zainab confronts Masood - is he a thief? Billie shows his love for Whitney, while Lucy faces the doctors alone.
New. Lucy stuns Jane with a shocking revelation, and Whitney and Billie Jackson face the reality of their new relationship when Bianca returns from honeymoon.
For Richer, for Poorer: Siegfried seizes an opportunity to show Callum 'the perfect operation' in another slice of vet drama set in a gentler age, starring the great Robert Hardy.
Tom and Jack do some male bonding whilst out fishing. Polly is concerned to learn that Gerald has been cashing cheques from their account; they are virtually bankrupt.
Thy Fury Spent: A simple case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre.
Substitute: More laughs with the Chigwell sisters. Tracey finds herself attracted to another man for the first time since her husband went to prison.
The Crowcroft Challenge: The world's longest-running, silliest sitcom! Alvin soon discovers that it isn't easy travelling as the crow flies, especially when dressed as a crow.
Must Be Good Dancer: More silver-haired shenanigans in rural Yorkshire. Smiler is known for being strictly non-dancing, but this presents a problem with the woman of his dreams.
Howard Remembers Where He Left His Bicycle Pump: Gentle comedy that first graced our screens in 1973. An embarrassed Howard bumps into a female acquaintance from his past.
Lost Youth: Martin Shaw is the heroic High Court judge. Deed is involved in a legal battle between parents and doctors over whether a seriously ill baby should be resuscitated.
Dockers: A fine ensemble cast, including Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman, star in the detective drama. The team probe the death of a unionist suspected of embezzlement.
Thy Fury Spent: A simple case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre.
Missing Joseph: The poisoning of a Lancashire vicar appears to be a tragic accident - until Lynley and Havers delve deeper into his past and discover a sinister motive for murder.
Baby Come Back: More jokes about Essex girls in the popular sitcom. When Sharon meets Dave, the telephone engineer and old flame, her thoughts turn to marriage and motherhood.
Good Heart and Willing Hand: Looming devastation to Five Points forces Corcoran to take drastic, irrevocable action. Injuries old and new push Doctor Matthew Freeman to his limit.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Just Family: Another episode of the classic Essex comedy. Tracey is hurt when Darryl asks her to give up one of her prison visits so that his mum and dad can go and see him.
Homework: Ackland's romantic evening away from Sun Hill spirals into something more sinister when a neighbour appears on her doorstep - carrying a shotgun.
New. Imitation of Life: Daniel and Julia both get a shock at a PCT meeting as he is recognised from his escort work. Vivien tries to prevent an old friend from being conned.
New. The Homecoming: Jimmi and Eva work together to retrieve an abducted baby and stop a woman from taking her own life.
Out of Control: Will Curtis breezes in as the new registrar, and George requires further surgery on the diseased heart given to him, presenting Ed and Tom with yet another moral dilemma.
Eliminate the Negative: Jordan tries to improve the image of the department. Jeff is hailed as a hero, but cannot handle being the centre of attention. Simone's mother antagonises Kirsty.
Homework: Ackland's romantic evening away from Sun Hill spirals into something more sinister when a neighbour appears on her doorstep - carrying a shotgun.
New. Billy Mitchell's love life takes a turn for the better when Carol agrees to go on a date with him, Masood makes a radical decision to prove his innocence.
New. Lucy offers Jane the greatest gift of all, while Becca makes Max a mysterious promise. Zsa Zsa receives disappointing news in the post, but finds an unlikely ally in George.
Against the Odds: The long-running and hugely popular drama series based on the veterinary memoirs of James Herriot. James tries to save a litter of piglets in the snow.
Leo's racing ambition comes to a drastic halt while Ken celebrates a success. Sir Edward gives Tom a business offer he can't refuse.
Reputation, Reputation, Reputation!: Frank and Lu enter the competitive world of hairdressing and find themselves combing through a web of deceit.
Sailing: More laughs with the Essex lasses. The prospect of a happy festive season looks bleak for Tracey and Sharon while their fellas are banged up at Her Majesty's pleasure.
Sinclair and the Wormley Witches: Senior silliness with the sprightly OAPs. Sinclair believes he's the victim of a curse. Truly, Entwistle and Alvin try their hand at exorcism.
Enter the Finger: The record-breaking sitcom that first came to our screens in 1973. Here, Brian Conley plays Barry's fitness-mad neighbour, who outshines him in all things physical.
Will the Genuine Racer Please Stand Up?: The young-at-heart sitcom about a group of retired friends. Howard devises a simple scheme to change his appearance enough to fool even Pearl.
Oysters Kilpatrick: Just as Pearl allows herself a weekend away with Tom, a food critic drops dead in the Whitstable Pearl, leaving her torn between staying or heading back.
Prisoners of the Past: Pearl is surprised to learn that Charlie is back from Canada and living in London, but her search for him leads her to uncover more about Mike's past.
Old Dogs: A fantastic cast of TV legends as the eccentric bunch of ex-policemen. A serial dog killer starts stalking local parks and the team are forced into taking on the case.
